pr_summary: Add bulk user fetch and fix password reset.
verdict_reason: The diff contains high-risk findings that should be fixed before merge.

findings.line_content: await db.query('UPDATE users SET password = ? WHERE email = ?',
findings.title: Password update stores un-hashed password input
findings.description: The password update writes password input without evidence of hashing.
findings.suggestion: Hash passwords with bcrypt or argon2 before storing them.

findings.title: Missing query parameter validation
findings.description: The code calls a string method on query input without a type check.
findings.suggestion: Validate the query parameter exists and is a string before parsing it.

findings.line_content: const user = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [id]);
findings.title: Missing test case for bulk fetch with no users
findings.description: The getUsers function does not handle the case where no users are returned from the database. This should be tested to ensure the correct response is returned.
findings.suggestion: Add a test case to verify the correct response is returned when no users are returned from the database

findings.line_content: const user = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [id]);
findings.title: Missing test case for invalid user ID
findings.description: The getUsers function does not handle the case where the user ID is invalid. This should be tested to ensure the correct error response is returned.
findings.suggestion: Add a test case to verify the correct error response is returned when the user ID is invalid

findings.title: Await inside loop serializes independent work
findings.description: Awaiting inside the loop makes independent operations run one after another instead of concurrently.
findings.suggestion: Use Promise.all or a bounded concurrency helper for independent work.

findings.title: N+1 query in loop
findings.description: The loop performs a data lookup per item, which creates N database or repository calls for N inputs.
findings.suggestion: Batch the lookup with an IN query or equivalent bulk API.

findings.title: Authorization check missing for sensitive operation
findings.description: The code performs a sensitive mutation without a visible ownership, role, or token validation check.
findings.suggestion: Check that the authenticated caller is allowed to perform this operation before changing state.

findings.title: Missing test case for user not found
findings.description: The resetPassword function does not handle the case where the user is not found. This should be tested to ensure the correct error response is returned.
findings.suggestion: Add a test case to verify the 404 response is returned when the user is not found

findings.title: Missing test case for user not found in activity logs
findings.description: The getUserActivity function does not handle the case where the user is not found in the activity logs. This should be tested to ensure the correct error response is returned.
findings.suggestion: Add a test case to verify the correct error response is returned when the user is not found in the activity logs

findings.title: Function name is not descriptive
findings.description: The function name 'getUsers' could be more descriptive. Consider renaming it to something like 'fetchUsersByIds' to improve code readability.

findings.title: Missing result handling
findings.description: The code uses the first result without checking whether a row exists.
findings.suggestion: Handle missing rows explicitly before adding them to the response.

findings.title: Function name is not descriptive
findings.description: The function name 'resetPassword' could be more descriptive. Consider renaming it to something like 'updateUserPassword' to improve code readability.

findings.line_content: const user = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [id]);
findings.title: Unclear variable name
findings.description: The variable name 'user' is not descriptive. Consider renaming it to something like 'foundUser' to improve code readability.

findings.title: Function name is not descriptive
findings.description: The function name 'getUserActivity' could be more descriptive. Consider renaming it to something like 'fetchUserActivityLogs' to improve code readability.

findings.line_content: const user = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?', [email]);
findings.title: Unclear variable name
findings.description: The variable name 'user' is not descriptive. Consider renaming it to something like 'foundUser' to improve code readability.

findings.title: Undefined related-record lookup in enrichment
findings.description: The enrichment references a related-record lookup that is not declared in the local scope, which can throw and often points to missing batch-loading.
findings.suggestion: Load related records explicitly and key them by ID before enrichment.

findings.title: Unclear variable name
findings.description: The variable name 'user' is not descriptive. Consider renaming it to something like 'foundUser' to improve code readability.

findings.line_content: // userController.js - PR: Add bulk fetch and fix password reset
findings.title: Comment is not necessary
findings.description: The comment is not necessary as the file name already indicates the purpose of the file.

positive_observations: The diff is small enough for a focused javascript line-level review.
The changes are organized around clear functions, which makes targeted fixes straightforward.
missing_tests: Add a test case to verify the correct response is returned when no users are returned from the database
Add a test case to verify the correct error response is returned when the user ID is invalid
Add a test case to verify the 404 response is returned when the user is not found
Add a test case to verify the correct error response is returned when the user is not found in the activity logs
